diagnosis: The player spent nearly two minutes stuck at the spawn point (x≈80, y≈754) before making any horizontal progress, then died three times to combat in the lower corridors (x≈96–560, y≈612–740). After finally climbing up through the ladder shaft near x≈470, they reached the right-side area but fell into a deep pit at the far right (x≈1440, y≈882) with no way to jump out, causing them to loop there for the last 30 seconds until time ran out. The level’s vertical ladder maze and deep, inescapable pit combined with early combat pressure made progress feel unfair and confusing.

lessons.lesson: Deep pits at the bottom of a level without any climb-out mechanic (ladders, ledges, or wall-jump surfaces) act as permanent fail states that trap players and force a restart.
conditions: pit depth exceeds maximum jump height
no vertical escape routes inside the pit
pit located on critical path or near exit
lessons.outcome: player falls in, cannot escape, loops at bottom until quitting or timing out

lessons.lesson: When a level opens with a dense cluster of enemies right at the spawn corridor, players who lack offensive upgrades or clear dodge space take repeated unavoidable damage before they can even orient themselves.
conditions: multiple enemies placed in the first horizontal corridor
player starts with minimal combat tools
lessons.outcome: early deaths, frustration, long stall at spawn before first meaningful movement
